pattern: Backtracking builds every non-decreasing subsequence while deduplicating choices at each level to avoid repeated sequences.
hints: Grow subsequences by appending only elements that are greater than or equal to the last one chosen.
At each recursion depth, avoid picking the same value twice from the remaining suffix, otherwise identical subsequences repeat.
Record any built sequence of length at least two; the order of the collected sequences in your result does not matter, but each sequence's own order does.

edge_inputs.description: two distinct values then a duplicate
strictly decreasing yields only the equal pair
single element has no length-two subsequence

stress.description: short arrays over a tiny value range to maximize duplicate subsequences
